journal_entry.description: Receipt of security deposit upon lease signing
journal_entry.rationale: Upon receiving a security deposit, cash (asset) increases and a liability is created because the deposit is refundable to the tenant at lease end (subject to any damages). The security deposit is NOT revenue - it represents a future obligation to return the funds. The tuples indicate one asset increase (cash received) and one liability increase (security deposit payable). No revenue is recognized at this time because the security deposit is refundable. The monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as revenue each month during the lease term, but that is not part of this initial transaction which only covers the security deposit receipt.

output.reason: Security deposit received creates an obligation to return it at lease end = liability increase (1 line). The cash received is the debit side (asset increase), not classified here.

credit_classifier.reason: Security deposit received creates an obligation to return it at lease end = liability increase (1 line). The cash received is the debit side (asset increase), not classified here.

output.reason: Mondelez receives $25,000 cash as a security deposit. This is an asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (obligation to return the deposit), which is handled on the credit side. Only 1 debit line: Cash (asset increase).
